bots.description: An array of botMetadata objects, with one entry for each bot.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, it includes a pagination token that you can specify in your next request to fetch the next page of bots.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot was updated. When you create a bot, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot was created.

version.description: The version of the bot. For a new bot, the version is always $LATEST.

intents.description: An array of builtinIntentMetadata objects, one for each intent in the response.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that fetches the next page of intents. If the response to this API call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of intents, specify the pagination token in the next request.

signature.description: A unique identifier for the built-in intent. To find the signature for an intent, see Standard Built-in Intents in the Alexa Skills Kit.

supportedLocales.description: A list of identifiers for the locales that the intent supports.

slotTypes.description: An array of BuiltInSlotTypeMetadata objects, one entry for each slot type returned.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, the response includes a pagination token that you can use in your next request to fetch the next page of slot types.

signature.description: A unique identifier for the built-in slot type. To find the signature for a slot type, see Slot Type Reference in the Alexa Skills Kit.

supportedLocales.description: A list of target locales for the slot.

intents.description: An array of Intent objects. For more information, see PutBot.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, the response includes a pagination token that you can specify in your next request to fetch the next page of intents.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the intent was updated. When you create an intent, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the intent was created.

migrationSummaries.description: An array of summaries for migrations from Amazon Lex V1 to Amazon Lex V2. To see details of the migration, use the migrationId from the summary in a call to the operation.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, it includes a pagination token that you can specify in your next request to fetch the next page of migrations.

migrationId.description: The unique identifier that Amazon Lex assigned to the migration.

v1BotName.description: The name of the Amazon Lex V1 bot that is the source of the migration.

v1BotVersion.description: The version of the Amazon Lex V1 bot that is the source of the migration.

v1BotLocale.description: The locale of the Amazon Lex V1 bot that is the source of the migration.

v2BotId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Lex V2 that is the destination of the migration.

v2BotRole.description: The IAM role that Amazon Lex uses to run the Amazon Lex V2 bot.

migrationStatus.description: The status of the operation. When the status is COMPLETE the bot is available in Amazon Lex V2. There may be alerts and warnings that need to be resolved to complete the migration.

migrationStrategy.description: The strategy used to conduct the migration.

migrationTimestamp.description: The date and time that the migration started.

slotTypes.description: An array of objects, one for each slot type, that provides information such as the name of the slot type, the version, and a description.

nextToken.description: If the response is truncated, it includes a pagination token that you can specify in your next request to fetch the next page of slot types.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the slot type was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the slot type was created.

intents.description: An array of Intent objects. For more information, see PutBot.

clarificationPrompt.description: The message that Amazon Lex uses when it doesn't understand the user's request. For more information, see PutBot.

abortStatement.description: The message that Amazon Lex uses to cancel a conversation. For more information, see PutBot.

status.description: When you send a request to create or update a bot, Amazon Lex sets the status response element to BUILDING. After Amazon Lex builds the bot, it sets status to READY. If Amazon Lex can't build the bot, it sets status to FAILED. Amazon Lex returns the reason for the failure in the failureReason response element.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex provides the reason that it failed to build the bot.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date when the $LATEST version of this bot was updated.

createdDate.description: The date when the bot version was created.

idleSessionTTLInSeconds.description: The maximum time in seconds that Amazon Lex retains the data gathered in a conversation. For more information, see PutBot.

voiceId.description: The Amazon Polly voice ID that Amazon Lex uses for voice interactions with the user.

checksum.description: Checksum identifying the version of the bot that was created.

locale.description: Specifies the target locale for the bot.

childDirected.description:
For each Amazon Lex bot created with the Amazon Lex Model Building Service, you must specify whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) by specifying true or false in the childDirected field. By specifying true in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. By specifying false in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is not related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. You may not specify a default value for the childDirected field that does not accurately reflect whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA.

If your use of Amazon Lex relates to a website, program, or other application that is directed in whole or in part, to children under age 13, you must obtain any required verifiable parental consent under COPPA. For information regarding the use of Amazon Lex in connection with websites, programs, or other applications that are directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13, see the Amazon Lex FAQ.

enableModelImprovements.description: Indicates whether the bot uses accuracy improvements. true indicates that the bot is using the improvements, otherwise, false.

detectSentiment.description: Indicates whether utterances entered by the user should be sent to Amazon Comprehend for sentiment analysis.

slots.description: An array of slot types that defines the information required to fulfill the intent.

sampleUtterances.description: An array of sample utterances configured for the intent.

confirmationPrompt.description: If defined, the prompt that Amazon Lex uses to confirm the user's intent before fulfilling it.

rejectionStatement.description: If the user answers "no" to the question defined in confirmationPrompt, Amazon Lex responds with this statement to acknowledge that the intent was canceled.

followUpPrompt.description: If defined, Amazon Lex uses this prompt to solicit additional user activity after the intent is fulfilled.

conclusionStatement.description: After the Lambda function specified in the fulfillmentActivity field fulfills the intent, Amazon Lex conveys this statement to the user.

dialogCodeHook.description: If defined, Amazon Lex invokes this Lambda function for each user input.

fulfillmentActivity.description: Describes how the intent is fulfilled.

parentIntentSignature.description: A unique identifier for a built-in intent.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the intent was updated.

createdDate.description: The date that the intent was created.

version.description: The version number assigned to the new version of the intent.

kendraConfiguration.description: Configuration information, if any, for connecting an Amazon Kendra index with the AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent intent.

inputContexts.description: An array of InputContext objects that lists the contexts that must be active for Amazon Lex to choose the intent in a conversation with the user.

outputContexts.description: An array of OutputContext objects that lists the contexts that the intent activates when the intent is fulfilled.

slotConstraint.description: Specifies whether the slot is required or optional.

slotType.description: The type of the slot, either a custom slot type that you defined or one of the built-in slot types.

valueElicitationPrompt.description: The prompt that Amazon Lex uses to elicit the slot value from the user.

messages.description: An array of objects, each of which provides a message string and its type. You can specify the message string in plain text or in Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML).

maxAttempts.description: The number of times to prompt the user for information.

responseCard.description: A response card. Amazon Lex uses this prompt at runtime, in the PostText API response. It substitutes session attributes and slot values for placeholders in the response card. For more information, see ex-resp-card.

priority.description:
Directs Amazon Lex the order in which to elicit this slot value from the user. For example, if the intent has two slots with priorities 1 and 2, AWS Amazon Lex first elicits a value for the slot with priority 1.

If multiple slots share the same priority, the order in which Amazon Lex elicits values is arbitrary.

sampleUtterances.description: If you know a specific pattern with which users might respond to an Amazon Lex request for a slot value, you can provide those utterances to improve accuracy. This is optional. In most cases, Amazon Lex is capable of understanding user utterances.

responseCard.description: A set of possible responses for the slot type used by text-based clients. A user chooses an option from the response card, instead of using text to reply.

obfuscationSetting.description: Determines whether a slot is obfuscated in conversation logs and stored utterances. When you obfuscate a slot, the value is replaced by the slot name in curly braces ({}). For example, if the slot name is "full_name", obfuscated values are replaced with "{full_name}". For more information, see Slot Obfuscation .

defaultValueSpec.description: A list of default values for the slot. Default values are used when Amazon Lex hasn't determined a value for a slot. You can specify default values from context variables, session attributes, and defined values.

defaultValueList.description:
The default values for a slot. You can specify more than one default. For example, you can specify a default value to use from a matching context variable, a session attribute, or a fixed value.

The default value chosen is selected based on the order that you specify them in the list. For example, if you specify a context variable and a fixed value in that order, Amazon Lex uses the context variable if it is available, else it uses the fixed value.

enumerationValues.description: A list of EnumerationValue objects that defines the values that the slot type can take.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the slot type was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last update date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the slot type was created.

version.description: The version assigned to the new slot type version.

checksum.description: Checksum of the $LATEST version of the slot type.

valueSelectionStrategy.description: The strategy that Amazon Lex uses to determine the value of the slot. For more information, see PutSlotType.

parentSlotTypeSignature.description: The built-in slot type used a the parent of the slot type.

slotTypeConfigurations.description: Configuration information that extends the parent built-in slot type.

synonyms.description: Additional values related to the slot type value.

intents.description: An array of intent objects. For more information, see PutBot.

enableModelImprovements.description: Indicates whether the bot uses accuracy improvements. true indicates that the bot is using the improvements, otherwise, false.

nluIntentConfidenceThreshold.description: The score that determines where Amazon Lex inserts the AMAZON.FallbackIntent, AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent, or both when returning alternative intents in a PostContent or PostText response. AMAZON.FallbackIntent is inserted if the confidence score for all intents is below this value. AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent is only inserted if it is configured for the bot.

clarificationPrompt.description: The message Amazon Lex uses when it doesn't understand the user's request. For more information, see PutBot.

abortStatement.description: The message that Amazon Lex returns when the user elects to end the conversation without completing it. For more information, see PutBot.

status.description:
The status of the bot.

When the status is BUILDING Amazon Lex is building the bot for testing and use.

If the status of the bot is READY_BASIC_TESTING, you can test the bot using the exact utterances specified in the bot's intents. When the bot is ready for full testing or to run, the status is READY.

If there was a problem with building the bot, the status is FAILED and the failureReason field explains why the bot did not build.

If the bot was saved but not built, the status is NOT_BUILT.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex explains why it failed to build the bot.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot was created.

idleSessionTTLInSeconds.description: The maximum time in seconds that Amazon Lex retains the data gathered in a conversation. For more information, see PutBot.

voiceId.description: The Amazon Polly voice ID that Amazon Lex uses for voice interaction with the user. For more information, see PutBot.

checksum.description: Checksum of the bot used to identify a specific revision of the bot's $LATEST version.

version.description: The version of the bot. For a new bot, the version is always $LATEST.

locale.description: The target locale for the bot.

childDirected.description:
For each Amazon Lex bot created with the Amazon Lex Model Building Service, you must specify whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) by specifying true or false in the childDirected field. By specifying true in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. By specifying false in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is not related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. You may not specify a default value for the childDirected field that does not accurately reflect whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA.

If your use of Amazon Lex relates to a website, program, or other application that is directed in whole or in part, to children under age 13, you must obtain any required verifiable parental consent under COPPA. For information regarding the use of Amazon Lex in connection with websites, programs, or other applications that are directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13, see the Amazon Lex FAQ.

detectSentiment.description: Indicates whether user utterances should be sent to Amazon Comprehend for sentiment analysis.

botVersion.description: The version of the bot that the alias points to.

botName.description: The name of the bot that the alias points to.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot alias was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and the last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot alias was created.

conversationLogs.description: The settings that determine how Amazon Lex uses conversation logs for the alias.

logSettings.description: The settings for your conversation logs. You can log text, audio, or both.

iamRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role used to write your logs to CloudWatch Logs or an S3 bucket.

BotAliases.description: An array of BotAliasMetadata objects, each describing a bot alias.

nextToken.description: A pagination token for fetching next page of aliases. If the response to this call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of aliases, specify the pagination token in the next request.

botVersion.description: The version of the Amazon Lex bot to which the alias points.

botName.description: The name of the bot to which the alias points.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot alias was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot alias was created.

conversationLogs.description: Settings that determine how Amazon Lex uses conversation logs for the alias.

logSettings.description: The settings for your conversation logs. You can log text, audio, or both.

iamRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role used to write your logs to CloudWatch Logs or an S3 bucket.

name.description: The name of the association between the bot and the channel.

description.description: A description of the association between the bot and the channel.

botAlias.description: An alias pointing to the specific version of the Amazon Lex bot to which this association is being made.

botName.description: The name of the Amazon Lex bot.

createdDate.description: The date that the association between the bot and the channel was created.

botConfiguration.description: Provides information that the messaging platform needs to communicate with the Amazon Lex bot.

status.description:
The status of the bot channel.

  • CREATED - The channel has been created and is ready for use.

  • IN_PROGRESS - Channel creation is in progress.

  • FAILED - There was an error creating the channel. For information about the reason for the failure, see the failureReason field.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex provides the reason that it failed to create the association.

botChannelAssociations.description: An array of objects, one for each association, that provides information about the Amazon Lex bot and its association with the channel.

nextToken.description: A pagination token that fetches the next page of associations. If the response to this call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of associations, specify the pagination token in the next request.

name.description: The name of the association between the bot and the channel.

description.description: A text description of the association you are creating.

botAlias.description: An alias pointing to the specific version of the Amazon Lex bot to which this association is being made.

botName.description:
The name of the Amazon Lex bot to which this association is being made.

Currently, Amazon Lex supports associations with Facebook and Slack, and Twilio.

createdDate.description: The date that the association between the Amazon Lex bot and the channel was created.

type.description: Specifies the type of association by indicating the type of channel being established between the Amazon Lex bot and the external messaging platform.

botConfiguration.description: Provides information necessary to communicate with the messaging platform.

status.description:
The status of the bot channel.

  • CREATED - The channel has been created and is ready for use.

  • IN_PROGRESS - Channel creation is in progress.

  • FAILED - There was an error creating the channel. For information about the reason for the failure, see the failureReason field.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex provides the reason that it failed to create the association.

bots.description: An array of BotMetadata objects, one for each numbered version of the bot plus one for the $LATEST version.

nextToken.description: A pagination token for fetching the next page of bot versions. If the response to this call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of versions, specify the pagination token in the next request.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot was updated. When you create a bot, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot was created.

version.description: The version of the bot. For a new bot, the version is always $LATEST.

signature.description: The unique identifier for a built-in intent.

supportedLocales.description: A list of locales that the intent supports.

slots.description: An array of BuiltinIntentSlot objects, one entry for each slot type in the intent.

name.description: The name of the bot being exported.

version.description: The version of the bot being exported.

exportStatus.description:
The status of the export.

  • IN_PROGRESS - The export is in progress.

  • READY - The export is complete.

  • FAILED - The export could not be completed.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex provides the reason that it failed to export the resource.

url.description: An S3 pre-signed URL that provides the location of the exported resource. The exported resource is a ZIP archive that contains the exported resource in JSON format. The structure of the archive may change. Your code should not rely on the archive structure.

name.description: The name given to the import job.

mergeStrategy.description: The action taken when there was a conflict between an existing resource and a resource in the import file.

importId.description: The identifier for the specific import job.

importStatus.description: The status of the import job. If the status is FAILED, you can get the reason for the failure from the failureReason field.

failureReason.description: A string that describes why an import job failed to complete.

createdDate.description: A timestamp for the date and time that the import job was created.

slots.description: An array of intent slots configured for the intent.

sampleUtterances.description: An array of sample utterances configured for the intent.

confirmationPrompt.description: If defined in the bot, Amazon Lex uses prompt to confirm the intent before fulfilling the user's request. For more information, see PutIntent.

rejectionStatement.description: If the user answers "no" to the question defined in confirmationPrompt, Amazon Lex responds with this statement to acknowledge that the intent was canceled.

followUpPrompt.description: If defined in the bot, Amazon Lex uses this prompt to solicit additional user activity after the intent is fulfilled. For more information, see PutIntent.

conclusionStatement.description: After the Lambda function specified in the fulfillmentActivity element fulfills the intent, Amazon Lex conveys this statement to the user.

dialogCodeHook.description: If defined in the bot, Amazon Amazon Lex invokes this Lambda function for each user input. For more information, see PutIntent.

fulfillmentActivity.description: Describes how the intent is fulfilled. For more information, see PutIntent.

parentIntentSignature.description: A unique identifier for a built-in intent.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the intent was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and the last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the intent was created.

kendraConfiguration.description: Configuration information, if any, to connect to an Amazon Kendra index with the AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent intent.

inputContexts.description: An array of InputContext objects that lists the contexts that must be active for Amazon Lex to choose the intent in a conversation with the user.

outputContexts.description: An array of OutputContext objects that lists the contexts that the intent activates when the intent is fulfilled.

slotConstraint.description: Specifies whether the slot is required or optional.

slotType.description: The type of the slot, either a custom slot type that you defined or one of the built-in slot types.

valueElicitationPrompt.description: The prompt that Amazon Lex uses to elicit the slot value from the user.

messages.description: An array of objects, each of which provides a message string and its type. You can specify the message string in plain text or in Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML).

maxAttempts.description: The number of times to prompt the user for information.

responseCard.description: A response card. Amazon Lex uses this prompt at runtime, in the PostText API response. It substitutes session attributes and slot values for placeholders in the response card. For more information, see ex-resp-card.

priority.description:
Directs Amazon Lex the order in which to elicit this slot value from the user. For example, if the intent has two slots with priorities 1 and 2, AWS Amazon Lex first elicits a value for the slot with priority 1.

If multiple slots share the same priority, the order in which Amazon Lex elicits values is arbitrary.

sampleUtterances.description: If you know a specific pattern with which users might respond to an Amazon Lex request for a slot value, you can provide those utterances to improve accuracy. This is optional. In most cases, Amazon Lex is capable of understanding user utterances.

responseCard.description: A set of possible responses for the slot type used by text-based clients. A user chooses an option from the response card, instead of using text to reply.

obfuscationSetting.description: Determines whether a slot is obfuscated in conversation logs and stored utterances. When you obfuscate a slot, the value is replaced by the slot name in curly braces ({}). For example, if the slot name is "full_name", obfuscated values are replaced with "{full_name}". For more information, see Slot Obfuscation .

defaultValueSpec.description: A list of default values for the slot. Default values are used when Amazon Lex hasn't determined a value for a slot. You can specify default values from context variables, session attributes, and defined values.

defaultValueList.description:
The default values for a slot. You can specify more than one default. For example, you can specify a default value to use from a matching context variable, a session attribute, or a fixed value.

The default value chosen is selected based on the order that you specify them in the list. For example, if you specify a context variable and a fixed value in that order, Amazon Lex uses the context variable if it is available, else it uses the fixed value.

intents.description: An array of IntentMetadata objects, one for each numbered version of the intent plus one for the $LATEST version.

nextToken.description: A pagination token for fetching the next page of intent versions. If the response to this call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of versions, specify the pagination token in the next request.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the intent was updated. When you create an intent, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the intent was created.

migrationId.description: The unique identifier of the migration. This is the same as the identifier used when calling the GetMigration operation.

v1BotName.description: The name of the Amazon Lex V1 bot migrated to Amazon Lex V2.

v1BotVersion.description: The version of the Amazon Lex V1 bot migrated to Amazon Lex V2.

v1BotLocale.description: The locale of the Amazon Lex V1 bot migrated to Amazon Lex V2.

v2BotId.description: The unique identifier of the Amazon Lex V2 bot that the Amazon Lex V1 is being migrated to.

v2BotRole.description: The IAM role that Amazon Lex uses to run the Amazon Lex V2 bot.

migrationStatus.description: Indicates the status of the migration. When the status is COMPLETE the migration is finished and the bot is available in Amazon Lex V2. There may be alerts and warnings that need to be resolved to complete the migration.

migrationStrategy.description:
The strategy used to conduct the migration.

  • CREATE_NEW - Creates a new Amazon Lex V2 bot and migrates the Amazon Lex V1 bot to the new bot.

  • UPDATE_EXISTING - Overwrites the existing Amazon Lex V2 bot metadata and the locale being migrated. It doesn't change any other locales in the Amazon Lex V2 bot. If the locale doesn't exist, a new locale is created in the Amazon Lex V2 bot.

migrationTimestamp.description: The date and time that the migration started.

alerts.description:
A list of alerts and warnings that indicate issues with the migration for the Amazon Lex V1 bot to Amazon Lex V2. You receive a warning when an Amazon Lex V1 feature has a different implementation if Amazon Lex V2.

For more information, see Migrating a bot in the Amazon Lex V2 developer guide.

type.description:
The type of alert. There are two kinds of alerts:

  • ERROR - There was an issue with the migration that can't be resolved. The migration stops.

  • WARN - There was an issue with the migration that requires manual changes to the new Amazon Lex V2 bot. The migration continues.

message.description: A message that describes why the alert was issued.

referenceURLs.description: A link to the Amazon Lex documentation that describes how to resolve the alert.

enumerationValues.description: A list of EnumerationValue objects that defines the values that the slot type can take.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the slot type was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last update date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the slot type was created.

checksum.description: Checksum of the $LATEST version of the slot type.

valueSelectionStrategy.description: The strategy that Amazon Lex uses to determine the value of the slot. For more information, see PutSlotType.

parentSlotTypeSignature.description: The built-in slot type used as a parent for the slot type.

slotTypeConfigurations.description: Configuration information that extends the parent built-in slot type.

synonyms.description: Additional values related to the slot type value.

slotTypes.description: An array of SlotTypeMetadata objects, one for each numbered version of the slot type plus one for the $LATEST version.

nextToken.description: A pagination token for fetching the next page of slot type versions. If the response to this call is truncated, Amazon Lex returns a pagination token in the response. To fetch the next page of versions, specify the pagination token in the next request.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the slot type was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the slot type was created.

botName.description: The name of the bot for which utterance information was returned.

utterances.description: An array of UtteranceList objects, each containing a list of UtteranceData objects describing the utterances that were processed by your bot. The response contains a maximum of 100 UtteranceData objects for each version. Amazon Lex returns the most frequent utterances received by the bot in the last 15 days.

botVersion.description: The version of the bot that processed the list.

utterances.description: One or more UtteranceData objects that contain information about the utterances that have been made to a bot. The maximum number of object is 100.

key.description: The key for the tag. Keys are not case-sensitive and must be unique.

value.description: The value associated with a key. The value may be an empty string but it can't be null.

intents.description: An array of Intent objects. For more information, see PutBot.

enableModelImprovements.description: Indicates whether the bot uses accuracy improvements. true indicates that the bot is using the improvements, otherwise, false.

nluIntentConfidenceThreshold.description: The score that determines where Amazon Lex inserts the AMAZON.FallbackIntent, AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent, or both when returning alternative intents in a PostContent or PostText response. AMAZON.FallbackIntent is inserted if the confidence score for all intents is below this value. AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent is only inserted if it is configured for the bot.

clarificationPrompt.description: The prompts that Amazon Lex uses when it doesn't understand the user's intent. For more information, see PutBot.

abortStatement.description: The message that Amazon Lex uses to cancel a conversation. For more information, see PutBot.

status.description:
When you send a request to create a bot with processBehavior set to BUILD, Amazon Lex sets the status response element to BUILDING.

In the READY_BASIC_TESTING state you can test the bot with user inputs that exactly match the utterances configured for the bot's intents and values in the slot types.

If Amazon Lex can't build the bot, Amazon Lex sets status to FAILED. Amazon Lex returns the reason for the failure in the failureReason response element.

When you set processBehavior to SAVE, Amazon Lex sets the status code to NOT BUILT.

When the bot is in the READY state you can test and publish the bot.

failureReason.description: If status is FAILED, Amazon Lex provides the reason that it failed to build the bot.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot was created.

idleSessionTTLInSeconds.description: The maximum length of time that Amazon Lex retains the data gathered in a conversation. For more information, see PutBot.

voiceId.description: The Amazon Polly voice ID that Amazon Lex uses for voice interaction with the user. For more information, see PutBot.

checksum.description: Checksum of the bot that you created.

version.description: The version of the bot. For a new bot, the version is always $LATEST.

locale.description: The target locale for the bot.

childDirected.description:
For each Amazon Lex bot created with the Amazon Lex Model Building Service, you must specify whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) by specifying true or false in the childDirected field. By specifying true in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. By specifying false in the childDirected field, you confirm that your use of Amazon Lex is not related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA. You may not specify a default value for the childDirected field that does not accurately reflect whether your use of Amazon Lex is related to a website, program, or other application that is directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13 and subject to COPPA.

If your use of Amazon Lex relates to a website, program, or other application that is directed in whole or in part, to children under age 13, you must obtain any required verifiable parental consent under COPPA. For information regarding the use of Amazon Lex in connection with websites, programs, or other applications that are directed or targeted, in whole or in part, to children under age 13, see the Amazon Lex FAQ.

createVersion.description: True if a new version of the bot was created. If the createVersion field was not specified in the request, the createVersion field is set to false in the response.

detectSentiment.description: true if the bot is configured to send user utterances to Amazon Comprehend for sentiment analysis. If the detectSentiment field was not specified in the request, the detectSentiment field is false in the response.

tags.description: A list of tags associated with the bot.

botVersion.description: The version of the bot that the alias points to.

botName.description: The name of the bot that the alias points to.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the bot alias was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and the last updated date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the bot alias was created.

checksum.description: The checksum for the current version of the alias.

conversationLogs.description: The settings that determine how Amazon Lex uses conversation logs for the alias.

tags.description: A list of tags associated with a bot.

key.description: The key for the tag. Keys are not case-sensitive and must be unique.

value.description: The value associated with a key. The value may be an empty string but it can't be null.

slots.description: An array of intent slots that are configured for the intent.

sampleUtterances.description: An array of sample utterances that are configured for the intent.

confirmationPrompt.description: If defined in the intent, Amazon Lex prompts the user to confirm the intent before fulfilling it.

rejectionStatement.description: If the user answers "no" to the question defined in confirmationPrompt Amazon Lex responds with this statement to acknowledge that the intent was canceled.

followUpPrompt.description: If defined in the intent, Amazon Lex uses this prompt to solicit additional user activity after the intent is fulfilled.

conclusionStatement.description: After the Lambda function specified in thefulfillmentActivityintent fulfills the intent, Amazon Lex conveys this statement to the user.

dialogCodeHook.description: If defined in the intent, Amazon Lex invokes this Lambda function for each user input.

fulfillmentActivity.description: If defined in the intent, Amazon Lex invokes this Lambda function to fulfill the intent after the user provides all of the information required by the intent.

parentIntentSignature.description: A unique identifier for the built-in intent that this intent is based on.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the intent was updated. When you create a resource, the creation date and last update dates are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the intent was created.

version.description: The version of the intent. For a new intent, the version is always $LATEST.

checksum.description: Checksum of the $LATESTversion of the intent created or updated.

createVersion.description: True if a new version of the intent was created. If the createVersion field was not specified in the request, the createVersion field is set to false in the response.

kendraConfiguration.description: Configuration information, if any, required to connect to an Amazon Kendra index and use the AMAZON.KendraSearchIntent intent.

inputContexts.description: An array of InputContext objects that lists the contexts that must be active for Amazon Lex to choose the intent in a conversation with the user.

outputContexts.description: An array of OutputContext objects that lists the contexts that the intent activates when the intent is fulfilled.

slotConstraint.description: Specifies whether the slot is required or optional.

slotType.description: The type of the slot, either a custom slot type that you defined or one of the built-in slot types.

valueElicitationPrompt.description: The prompt that Amazon Lex uses to elicit the slot value from the user.

messages.description: An array of objects, each of which provides a message string and its type. You can specify the message string in plain text or in Speech Synthesis Markup Language (SSML).

maxAttempts.description: The number of times to prompt the user for information.

responseCard.description: A response card. Amazon Lex uses this prompt at runtime, in the PostText API response. It substitutes session attributes and slot values for placeholders in the response card. For more information, see ex-resp-card.

priority.description:
Directs Amazon Lex the order in which to elicit this slot value from the user. For example, if the intent has two slots with priorities 1 and 2, AWS Amazon Lex first elicits a value for the slot with priority 1.

If multiple slots share the same priority, the order in which Amazon Lex elicits values is arbitrary.

sampleUtterances.description: If you know a specific pattern with which users might respond to an Amazon Lex request for a slot value, you can provide those utterances to improve accuracy. This is optional. In most cases, Amazon Lex is capable of understanding user utterances.

responseCard.description: A set of possible responses for the slot type used by text-based clients. A user chooses an option from the response card, instead of using text to reply.

obfuscationSetting.description: Determines whether a slot is obfuscated in conversation logs and stored utterances. When you obfuscate a slot, the value is replaced by the slot name in curly braces ({}). For example, if the slot name is "full_name", obfuscated values are replaced with "{full_name}". For more information, see Slot Obfuscation .

defaultValueSpec.description: A list of default values for the slot. Default values are used when Amazon Lex hasn't determined a value for a slot. You can specify default values from context variables, session attributes, and defined values.

defaultValueList.description:
The default values for a slot. You can specify more than one default. For example, you can specify a default value to use from a matching context variable, a session attribute, or a fixed value.

The default value chosen is selected based on the order that you specify them in the list. For example, if you specify a context variable and a fixed value in that order, Amazon Lex uses the context variable if it is available, else it uses the fixed value.

enumerationValues.description: A list of EnumerationValue objects that defines the values that the slot type can take.

lastUpdatedDate.description: The date that the slot type was updated. When you create a slot type, the creation date and last update date are the same.

createdDate.description: The date that the slot type was created.

version.description: The version of the slot type. For a new slot type, the version is always $LATEST.

checksum.description: Checksum of the $LATEST version of the slot type.

valueSelectionStrategy.description: The slot resolution strategy that Amazon Lex uses to determine the value of the slot. For more information, see PutSlotType.

createVersion.description: True if a new version of the slot type was created. If the createVersion field was not specified in the request, the createVersion field is set to false in the response.

parentSlotTypeSignature.description: The built-in slot type used as the parent of the slot type.

slotTypeConfigurations.description: Configuration information that extends the parent built-in slot type.

synonyms.description: Additional values related to the slot type value.

name.description: The name given to the import job.

mergeStrategy.description: The action to take when there is a merge conflict.

importId.description: The identifier for the specific import job.

importStatus.description: The status of the import job. If the status is FAILED, you can get the reason for the failure using the GetImport operation.

tags.description: A list of tags added to the imported bot.

createdDate.description: A timestamp for the date and time that the import job was requested.

key.description: The key for the tag. Keys are not case-sensitive and must be unique.

value.description: The value associated with a key. The value may be an empty string but it can't be null.

v1BotName.description: The name of the Amazon Lex V1 bot that you are migrating to Amazon Lex V2.

v1BotVersion.description: The version of the bot to migrate to Amazon Lex V2.

v1BotLocale.description: The locale used for the Amazon Lex V1 bot.

v2BotId.description: The unique identifier for the Amazon Lex V2 bot.

v2BotRole.description: The IAM role that Amazon Lex uses to run the Amazon Lex V2 bot.

migrationId.description: The unique identifier that Amazon Lex assigned to the migration.

migrationStrategy.description: The strategy used to conduct the migration.

migrationTimestamp.description: The date and time that the migration started.
